regions.name: Sakai
regions.summary: Specialized production chains: smiths, sharpeners, brands and retailers often all matter.
notes: Use Sakai as a chain-reading region: who forged it, who sharpened it, which line, which retailer context.
The blacksmith and sharpener relationship is often the point, not a footnote.

nodes.name: Ikeda
nodes.specialty: Sakai carbon forging, traditional single-bevel work and honyaki-adjacent buying context.
nodes.caveat: Exact first name and line attribution should be checked listing by listing.

nodes.name: Nomura
nodes.specialty: Sakai sharpening, bevel geometry and final cutting feel.
nodes.caveat: Added as the Ikeda/Nomura relationship example requested by Adrian; keep exact product claims conservative.

nodes.name: Kenichi Shiraki
nodes.specialty: Sakai carbon forging lineage, honyaki capability and apprenticeship influence.

nodes.name: Satoshi Nakagawa
nodes.specialty: Ginsan, Aogami, Shirogami and honyaki-capable Sakai forging.

nodes.name: Ashi Hamono
nodes.specialty: Thin Sakai laser geometry and clean monosteel performance.

nodes.name: Mitsuaki Takada
nodes.specialty: Thin convex hamaguri-style grinding, Suiboku finish and modern Sakai polish.

nodes.name: Konosuke
nodes.specialty: Sakai curation where smith, sharpener and exact line define the knife.

nodes.name: Morihiro
nodes.specialty: Reference-point Sakai sharpening identity behind original Fujiyama context.

nodes.name: HADO / Fukui
nodes.specialty: Modern Sakai presentation with named craft, Ginsan lines and clean finishing.

nodes.name: Baba Hamono
nodes.specialty: Sakai maker network and Kagekiyo house identity.

edges.label: forging to sharpening
edges.detail: Ikeda is treated as the blacksmith node and Nomura as the sharpener node in the Sakai chain example.

edges.label: apprenticeship lineage
edges.detail: Nakagawa is commonly described as training under Shiraki before founding his own Sakai workshop.

edges.label: Ashi background
edges.detail: Takada's profile is tied to years at Ashi Hamono before founding Takada no Hamono.

edges.label: Fujiyama sharpening identity
edges.detail: Konosuke's Fujiyama/FM story makes sharpener attribution part of the buyer conversation.

edges.label: modern Sakai brand context
edges.detail: Both show how modern Sakai brands make specialist craft legible to buyers.

regions.name: Sanjo
regions.summary: Blacksmith-forward shops, robust convexity and workhorse identities.
notes: Sanjo is useful for understanding taller, more powerful double-bevel knives.
Relationships here often look like workshop training or regional influence rather than a Sakai-style smith/sharpener split.

nodes.name: Yoshikane Hamono
nodes.specialty: Sanjo convex grinds, SKD, White #2 and controlled workhorse geometry.

nodes.name: Toshihiro Wakui
nodes.specialty: Small-shop Sanjo carbon knives with Yoshikane influence.

nodes.name: Syuuji Toyama
nodes.specialty: Handmade Sanjo carbon knives, strong food-moving geometry and JNS Noborikoi context.

nodes.name: Masashi Yamamoto
nodes.specialty: Sanjo profile language, distinctive grinds and visually expressive lines.

edges.label: Yoshikane influence
edges.detail: Wakui profiles are commonly framed through time at Yoshikane and Sanjo tool-making context.

edges.label: Sanjo workhorse comparison
edges.detail: Yoshikane and Toyama are useful comparison points for Sanjo power, convexity and carbon care.

edges.label: Sanjo maker context
edges.detail: Both help show that Sanjo is not one grind; it is a region with different shop identities.

regions.name: Echizen
regions.summary: Takefu-style maker networks, laminated steels and visually distinct modern lines.
notes: Echizen/Takefu is useful as a cooperative-region map: several recognizable makers share regional infrastructure and training context.
Use exact maker and line before assuming a single Echizen cutting feel.

nodes.name: Takefu Knife Village
nodes.specialty: Regional maker hub for Echizen forged knives and shared craft visibility.

nodes.name: Yu Kurosaki
nodes.specialty: Highly recognizable hammered and patterned finishes with modern export visibility.

nodes.name: Takeshi Saji
nodes.specialty: Expressive Damascus, western handles, R2/SG2 and outdoor-leaning visual language.

nodes.name: Shiro Kamo
nodes.specialty: Accessible handmade carbon and stainless-clad Echizen knives.

nodes.name: Yoshimi Kato
nodes.specialty: Damascus, SG2/R2 and clean modern Echizen lines.

edges.label: Takefu network
edges.detail: Kurosaki is best read inside the broader Takefu/Echizen maker network.

edges.label: Takefu network
edges.detail: Saji is a senior Echizen reference point for expressive Damascus and modern lines.

edges.label: Takefu network
edges.detail: Shiro Kamo helps show the accessible handmade carbon side of the region.

edges.label: Takefu network
edges.detail: Kato belongs in the same regional network but should still be read by exact line and steel.

regions.name: Miki / Hyogo
regions.summary: A non-Sakai example slot for Tanaka Uchihamono and the Kawakita relationship request.
notes: This region is included because Adrian specifically asked for the Tanaka Uchihamono and Kawakita relationship.
The graph marks the relationship without overstating exact line names until a stronger source is added.

nodes.name: Tanaka Uchihamono
nodes.specialty: Forged kitchen knives; read by exact smith, line, grind and retailer details.
nodes.caveat: Relationship included from Adrian's requested example; add exact retailer/source link when available.

nodes.name: Kawakita
nodes.specialty: Listed as the relationship counterpart for Tanaka Uchihamono in this map.
nodes.caveat: Keep this node conservative until a source trail is attached.

edges.label: working relationship
edges.detail: Added to make the Tanaka Uchihamono / Kawakita relationship visible as requested.

sources.label: Sakai Ichimonji: How Japanese knives are made
Japan House London: Sakai forged knives
